ui.realAlignmentFallback: Real data failed · using simulation coordinates

descriptions.visualScale: Readable distance and radius compression for navigation.
descriptions.compressedRealScale: Linear AU spacing with boosted planet radii.
descriptions.realScalePlaceholder: True-scale draft showing why exact scale is difficult.

info.emptyBody: Select a body to inspect its mission data.

flight.readyBody: The cursor will lock to the canvas. Use mouse look after launch.
flight.switchingBody: Camera and input state are being synchronized.
flight.pausedBody: Cursor control is released. Resume to lock the canvas again, or return to observatory controls.

errors.pointerLockBlockedLaunch: Pointer lock was blocked. Click the launch overlay or canvas again.
errors.pointerLockBlockedResume: Pointer lock was blocked. Click Resume Flight again.
errors.pointerLockUnsupported: This browser does not expose pointer lock for the canvas.

sun.name: Sun
sun.description: The star anchoring the solar system and the main light source of the scene.

mercury.name: Mercury
mercury.description: The smallest planet, orbiting closest to the Sun.

venus.name: Venus
venus.description: A hot terrestrial world wrapped in a dense golden atmosphere.

earth.name: Earth
earth.description: The reference world for scale, clouds, atmosphere, and mission navigation.

mars.name: Mars
mars.description: A cold rocky planet with a thin atmosphere and dusty red terrain.

jupiter.name: Jupiter
jupiter.description: The largest planet, prepared for banded texture and storm detail.

saturn.name: Saturn
saturn.description: A gas giant with a wide ring system and future moon expansion.

uranus.name: Uranus
uranus.description: A tilted ice giant with a quiet cyan atmosphere.

neptune.name: Neptune
neptune.description: A distant ice giant with deep blue atmospheric scattering.

moon.name: Moon
moon.description: Earth's primary moon, ready for future ephemeris replacement.

phobos.name: Phobos
phobos.description: Inner Martian moon prepared for parent-child orbit rendering.

deimos.name: Deimos
deimos.description: Outer Martian moon prepared for parent-child orbit rendering.

io.name: Io
io.description: Galilean moon placeholder with approximate orbital distance.

europa.name: Europa
europa.description: Icy Galilean moon reserved for future ocean-world data.

ganymede.name: Ganymede
ganymede.description: The largest moon in the solar system, included as a high-priority satellite.

callisto.name: Callisto
callisto.description: Outer Galilean moon used to validate nested satellite systems.

titan.name: Titan
titan.description: Saturn's flagship moon with atmosphere data reserved for later passes.

enceladus.name: Enceladus
enceladus.description: Icy Saturnian moon reserved for plume and surface detail.

rhea.name: Rhea
rhea.description: Saturnian moon included for medium and high quality satellite density.

iapetus.name: Iapetus
iapetus.description: Distant Saturnian moon useful for hierarchy and label testing.

mimas.name: Mimas
mimas.description: Small inner Saturnian moon for high quality passes.

miranda.name: Miranda
miranda.description: Small Uranian moon for high quality passes.

triton.name: Triton
triton.description: Neptune's primary moon with retrograde orbit placeholder data.

pluto.name: Pluto
pluto.description: Dwarf planet included as the first Kuiper Belt expansion target.

ceres.name: Ceres
ceres.description: The largest object in the asteroid belt, modeled as a dwarf planet.

eris.name: Eris
eris.description: Distant dwarf planet reserved for scattered disk expansion.

haumea.name: Haumea
haumea.description: Fast-spinning dwarf planet placeholder with shape modeling reserved.
